synthesis.executiveSummary: Decision rights remain the strongest cross-session theme.

crossInterviewThemes.title: Decision bottlenecks
crossInterviewThemes.summary: Approvals stall because ownership is split across multiple teams.

evidence.rationale: Liam echoed the same delay pattern.

positions: Central operations should keep final sign-off for exceptions.
Regional teams need delegated authority for routine cases.

evidence.rationale: The respondent contrasted regional speed with central sign-off.

notableQuotesByTheme.label: Approval frustration
notableQuotesByTheme.summary: "We do not need another framework; we need a way to stop waiting on three approvals."

notableQuotesByTheme.label: Missing evidence
notableQuotesByTheme.summary: This claim should not fabricate text when transcript rows are missing.

evidence.rationale: The cited row no longer exists.

transcript.speaker: participant
transcript.text: We need clearer owners when an exception lands between regional and central teams.

transcript.speaker: participant
transcript.text: We do not need another framework; we need a way to stop waiting on three approvals.

transcript.speaker: participant
transcript.text: Routine exceptions should move faster in-region.

transcript.speaker: participant
transcript.text: Central operations still wants final sign-off when policy rules conflict.

transcript.speaker: participant
transcript.text: Every exception seems to wait for a second approval before anything moves.
